frameworks.name: bounded accumulation model
frameworks.one_line: decision as noisy evidence accumulation to a bound setting speed-accuracy tradeoff

frameworks.name: decision confidence
frameworks.one_line: post-decisional confidence used to update next-trial decision bound

core_question: Does post-decisional confidence on one trial adjust the speed-accuracy tradeoff (decision bound) on the next trial?
key_assumptions: no external feedback available, so agents rely on internal confidence
speed-accuracy tradeoff is set by the height of the decision bound
lower confidence on trial n motivates more cautious accumulation on trial n+1
manipulations: reported confidence about correctness of the previous choice
three different perceptual binary-choice tasks with subsequent confidence rating
post-decisional EEG components (error positivity vs error-related negativity)
key_findings: fitted decision bounds depended on previous-trial confidence
bound increase was strongest after high subjective certainty of an error
centro-parietal error positivity predicted next-trial bound increase
earlier mid-frontal ERN did not predict bound increase
interpretations: confidence serves as an internal teaching signal that continuously regulates the speed-accuracy tradeoff
error positivity, not ERN, indexes the confidence signal driving policy adjustment
